title: Warming Saudi Egg Flatbread
description: A fluffy, wine-infused flatbread kissed with sweet smoked paprika, perfect for a romantic breakfast or brunch.
instructions: Combine the flour, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Whisk together the eggs, milk, wine, and olive oil in a separate bowl.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a smooth dough forms.
Let the dough rest for 10 minutes to relax the gluten.
Divide the dough into four equal portions and roll each into a thin circle about 6 inches in diameter.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and brush lightly with olive oil.
Place one flatbread in the skillet and cook until bubbles form on the surface, about 2 minutes.
Flip the flatbread and cook the other side until golden, about 1 minute.
Fold the cooked flatbread in half, press down lightly, and transfer to a warm plate.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped parsley and a drizzle of honey if desired.
